title: 10 best herbal teas for acne

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Germander tea has antimicrobial properties that help combat acne-causing bacteria like Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) on the skin, reducing inflammation and preventing further breakouts.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in germander tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with acne, and also help soothe irritated skin.

Antioxidant properties: Germander tea is rich in antioxidants that neutralize free radicals, which can damage skin cells and contribute to acne. Antioxidants also help promote healthy skin cell turnover, reducing the appearance of acne scars.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Teucrium chamaedrys, also known as germander, teas helps with acne because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The tea's active compounds, such as teucrin and teucridin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and combat acne-causing bacteria, like Propionibacterium acnes. By regulating the balance of the skin's natural flora and reducing inflammation, germander tea can help to prevent clogged pores, reduce redness, and promote a clear and healthy complexion. This makes it a popular natural remedy for acne-prone skin.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinolic acid: This labdane diterpenoid has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne.
Oleanolic acid: This triterpenoid has antimicrobial properties, which can help control the growth of bacteria that can cause acne, such as Propionibacterium acnes.

Flavonoids (including ursolic acid): These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the skin, leading to fewer acne breakouts.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make teas for acne due to their antiseptic and antibacterial properties.
Buds: They are used to make teas for acne due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing.
Leaves: They are also used to make teas for acne due to their content of phenolic compounds that exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1/4 cup of dried teucrium chamaedrys leaves and flowers, clean them thoroughly to remove any debris.
Measure out 1 teaspoon of the dried herb for every 8 ounces of water, then place them in a teapot.
Steep the herb in boiling water for 5-10 minutes, allowing the flavors to infuse fully.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids from the liquid.
Serve the teucrium chamaedrys tea hot or chilled, sweetened with honey or sugar to taste.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce inflammation in the skin, which can exacerbate acne.

Antimicrobial properties: The tea's antimicrobial agents, including luteolin and apigenin, work to combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria, a common cause of acne, by inhibiting their growth and activity.
Antioxidant properties: Pot marigold tea's high antioxidant content helps protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to acne development and severity.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, teas helps with acne because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The tea's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and carotenoids, work to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne. Additionally, the tea's antiseptic properties help to combat bacterial overgrowth, which can contribute to acne development. By regulating the skin's natural balance and reducing inflammation, Calendula officinalis tea can promote a clearer complexion and alleviate acne symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids found in Calendula officinalis have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help to reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth that can cause acne.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Calendula officinalis have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which help to protect the skin from oxidative stress and prevent bacterial infections that can lead to acne.

Saponins: Saponins in Calendula officinalis have anti-inflammatory and wound-healing properties, which help to reduce inflammation and promote the healing of acne lesions, thereby preventing further damage and scarring.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe and calm acne-prone skin.
Leaves: They have antiseptic and antifungal properties that aid in reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin.
Seeds: They are high in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and inflammation associated with acne.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the calendula officinalis flowers at their peak bloom, typically in summer months when they are most vibrant.
Rinse the calendula flowers thoroughly with cold running water to remove dirt and debris.
Combine 1 tablespoon of dried calendula flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 7 minutes to allow the herbal properties to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea into a separate container using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Dandelion tea contains antioxidants like flavonoids and phenolic acids that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are major contributors to acne development.

Anti-Inflammatory Effects: The anti-inflammatory compounds in dandelion tea, such as kaempferol and quercetin, can help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Detoxifying Properties: Dandelion tea is rich in prebiotic fiber that can help regulate gut bacteria, reducing toxins and waste products that can clog pores and cause acne.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, teas helps with acne because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The roots and leaves of the dandelion plant are rich in antioxidants, which help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with acne. Additionally, dandelion teas have been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which help to combat bacterial overgrowth that can contribute to the development of acne. These properties make dandelion teas a natural and effective remedy for acne-prone skin.
remedy_constituents: Inulin: Helps with acne by reducing inflammation and soothing skin irritation due to its prebiotic properties, which promote a healthy gut-skin axis.
Luteolin: Helps with acne by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, which contribute to acne development and exacerbation.
Kaempferol: Helps with acne by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce oxidative stress and inflammation that can cause acne lesions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of antioxidants and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties to help soothe acne.
Flowers: They are used because of their antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which help to reduce acne-causing bacteria and inflammation.
Roots: They are used due to their ability to stimulate the immune system and reduce inflammation, making them effective in treating acne.
remedy_recipe: Choose fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers and clean them thoroughly with cold water to remove dirt.
Steep one teaspoon of taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in one cup of boiling water for five minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove taraxacum officinale solids.
Add honey or other natural sweeteners to taste if desired to balance taraxacum officinale flavor.
Serve the taraxacum officinale tea hot or chilled immediately after preparation to maximize benefits.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chamomile tea contains apigenin and luteolin, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, promoting a smoother skin complexion.

Antimicrobial activity: The flavonoids present in chamomile tea, such as apigenin and quercetin, exhibit antimicrobial properties that can help combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria, which can contribute to the development of acne.

Calming and soothing effects: Chamomile tea's calming and soothing properties can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are known triggers for acne. By promoting relaxation, chamomile tea may also help regulate hormonal imbalances that can lead to acne.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, teas helps with acne because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The apigenin and bisabolol present in chamomile have been shown to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne. Additionally, chamomile tea's antioxidant properties help to protect the skin from environmental stressors and promote healthy skin cell regeneration. This can lead to reduced acne severity and faster healing times, resulting in clearer, more balanced skin. It also helps to calm the skin, reducing the appearance of acne and promoting a smoother complexion.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: Apigenin is a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of bacteria that cause acne.
Luteolin: Luteolin is a flavonoid that has potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent the spread of acne-causing bacteria.
Bisabolol: Bisabolol is a sesquiterpene that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with acne.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help soothe and calm acne-prone skin.
Leaves: Contain antioxidants and flavonoids that can help reduce inflammation and prevent further breakouts.
(Optional) Seeds: Although less commonly mentioned, some sources suggest that the seeds may have anti-inflammatory properties that can aid in acne treatment.
remedy_recipe: Choose 1 tablespoon of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers or 2 to 3 teaspoons of fresh flowers for every 8 ounces of water.
Heat water to a temperature of 212 degrees fahrenheit in a teapot or kettle for 5 minutes.
Steep the matricaria chamomilla flowers in the hot water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup for 2 minutes.
Serve the tea hot or cold and consume within 24 hours for best flavor and potency.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne by decreasing inflammation in the skin.

Antibacterial properties: The antibacterial compounds present in aloe vera tea, including salicylic acid and lupeol, work to combat the bacterial growth that can cause acne by inhibiting the multiplication of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria.

Wound healing and antioxidant properties: Aloe vera's wound-healing compounds, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, help promote skin repair and regeneration, reducing the appearance of acne scars and promoting a faster recovery from breakouts.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, teas helps with acne because of its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel extracted from aloe vera contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and combat bacteria that can cause breakouts. Additionally, aloe vera tea has been found to balance the skin's natural pH and promote the healing process, reducing redness and promoting clear skin. This natural remedy can be a valuable addition to any acne treatment routine.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth that leads to acne.
Aloin and aloe-emodin: These anthraquinones have antimicrobial properties, which can inhibit the growth of bacteria such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a primary cause of acne.

Apolypodol (Aloe-emodin glucoside): This compound has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help reduce redness and inflammation associated with acne, as well as prevent the spread of bacteria.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for making teas due to their high content of aloe-emodin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce acne.
Stems: Used for making teas due to their high content of aloe-aceroside, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can help soothe acne-prone skin.
Flowers: Used for making teas due to their high content of aloin, a compound with antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce acne-causing bacteria and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Wash your hands thoroughly with soap and water before handling aloe vera plants.
Harvest a 10-inch piece of aloe vera leaf from a mature plant and cut off the yellowing edges.
Peel off the outer skin of the aloe vera leaf and chop the gel into small cubes.
Mix 1 cup of the chopped aloe vera gel with 4 cups of boiling water in a saucepan and let it steep for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup and serve warm or chilled.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, making it an effective natural remedy for calming skin irritations.

Antibacterial properties: The antimicrobial agents in licorice tea, including triterpenes and phenolic acids, have been shown to inhibit the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a bacteria that contributes to acne formation.

Hormone-balancing properties: Licorice root has been found to regulate hormone levels, particularly cortisol, which can contribute to acne development by stimulating oil production and inflammation in the skin.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, teas helps with acne because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The flavonoids and saponins present in licorice root have been shown to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that cause acne. Additionally, licorice root has been found to have antioxidant properties, which help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, licorice root teas can help to clear up acne and promote healthy, glowing skin.
remedy_constituents: Licorice Isoflavones: These compounds help with acne by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to the development and progression of acne.
Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, making it a beneficial component in acne treatment.

Glycosides (specifically, Liquiritin): Liquiritin has been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the appearance of acne and preventing further inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are commonly used to make teas for acne due to their high glycyrrhizin content, which has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help soothe and calm acne-prone skin.

Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are also used to make teas for acne because they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing of acne lesions.

Flowers: The flowers of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make teas for acne due to their ability to reduce inflammation and fight off bacterial infections that can cause acne, thanks to their content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots with a minimum of 6% glycyrrhizin content by weight.
Boil 1 liter of water for 5 minutes to prepare a hot water infusion.
Steep 5 grams of the dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine mesh to remove the roots and discard them.
Store the tea in the refrigerator for up to 2 days and serve chilled or at room temperature.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ironwort tea is rich in antioxidants, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by acne, thus preventing further damage to the skin.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in Ironwort tea can reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with acne, allowing for faster healing and reduced scarring.
Antibacterial properties: Ironwort tea's antibacterial properties help combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), the bacteria that causes acne, by inhibiting its growth and reducing inflammation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sideritis scardica, also known as ironwort, teas helps with acne because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The tea's active compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, reduce inflammation and fight off bacteria that cause breakouts. Its antioxidant properties also help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation. By promoting a healthy balance of gut bacteria, Sideritis scardica tea can also help to prevent future acne outbreaks, leading to clearer and smoother skin.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic Acid: A polyphenolic compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with acne.
Caffeic Acid: A phenolic compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of acne-causing bacteria.
Bornyl Acetate: A terpene with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of acne-causing bacteria, as well as soothe and calm the skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used in teas for acne due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and prevent damage to skin cells.
Flowers: Used in teas for acne due to their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help combat acne-causing bacteria and reduce redness and inflammation.
Buds: Used in teas for acne due to their high antioxidant content, which helps protect the skin from environmental stressors and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sideritis scardica flowers and leaves when in full bloom and at their peak potency.
Dry the harvested sideritis scardica flowers and leaves in a warm and dry place for 1 week.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried sideritis scardica flowers and leaves for every 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the dried sideritis scardica mixture in boiling water for 5-10 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the tea and let it cool to room temperature before serving.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Kansas Coneflower tea has anti-inflammatory compounds like kaempferol, quercetin, and luteolin, which can reduce redness and swelling associated with acne.

Antibacterial properties: The tea's antibacterial properties, thanks to the presence of sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, can help combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria that contribute to acne.

Antioxidant properties: Kansas Coneflower tea's antioxidant compounds, such as gallic acid and ellagic acid, can help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and lead to premature aging and inflammation, which can worsen acne.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia, also known as Kansas coneflower, teas helps with acne because it has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling associated with acne. The tea's antimicrobial properties also help to combat bacterial overgrowth, which can contribute to breakouts. Additionally, Echinacea's antioxidant properties help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall skin health and reducing the appearance of acne. This makes Echinacea angustifolia tea a popular natural remedy for acne-prone skin.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: Iridoid glycosides in Echinacea angustifolia, such as echinacoside and iso-echinacoside, possess anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and inflammation associated with acne.

Alkaloids: Alkaloids present in Echinacea angustifolia, including echinatin and echinacoside, exhibit antimicrobial and antiseptic properties that help combat acne-causing bacteria and reduce the severity of acne.

Phenolic Acids: Phenolic acids, including caffeic acid and ferulic acid, in Echinacea angustifolia possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect the skin from oxidative stress and inflammation, contributing to the prevention and treatment of acne.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are high in inulin, a prebiotic fiber that supports gut health, which may help alleviate acne symptoms.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties to reduce redness and inflammation associated with acne.
Roots: They also contain alkylamides, which have immunomodulatory effects, helping to regulate the immune system and reduce acne inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea angustifolia roots in late summer or early fall when the plant is flowering to ensure maximum potency.
Clean the harvested roots thoroughly with water to remove dirt and debris from the surface.
Dry the clean roots in a warm dark place at 30-40 degrees celsius for 2-3 weeks to prevent mold.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and use 1 teaspoon per 8 oz of boiling water for tea preparation.
Steep the chopped roots in boiling water for 5-10 minutes to allow the herbal properties to infuse into the tea.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Agrimony tea has been shown to exhibit antibacterial activity against Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a bacteria commonly associated with acne, helping to reduce inflammation and prevent further breakouts.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in Agrimony tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness, swelling, and pain associated with acne.

Antioxidant properties: Agrimony tea is rich in antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals, which can damage skin cells and contribute to acne. By scavenging for these damaging compounds, antioxidants can help protect the skin from oxidative stress.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Agrimonia eupatoria, also known as agrimony, teas helps with acne because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The tea contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth that can lead to acne. Additionally, agrimony's antiseptic properties help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and promoting a healthy balance of skin flora. This makes it an effective natural remedy for acne-prone skin, helping to clear up breakouts and prevent future occurrences.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: These help reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth, which can contribute to acne.
Tannins: They have astringent properties that help reduce sebum production and prevent clogged pores, common causes of acne.
Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin): This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce acne inflammation and protect the skin from damage.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves of Agrimonia eupatoria are used to make teas due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm acne-prone skin.
Flowers: Flowers of Agrimonia eupatoria are used to make teas due to their antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which help combat acne-causing bacteria.
Barks: Barks of Agrimonia eupatoria are used to make teas due to their astringent properties, which help reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth in acne-prone skin.
remedy_recipe: Gather agrimonia eupatoria leaves and flowers from a trusted source in amounts of 1-2 teaspoons per 8 ounces water.
Rinse the gathered agrimonia eupatoria thoroughly with fresh water to remove any debris.
Heat 8 ounces of filtered water in a saucepan to a temperature of 212 degrees fahrenheit.
Steep the agrimonia eupatoria leaves and flowers in the heated water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Mullein tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne by reducing inflammation in the skin.
Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in mullein tea help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to acne development by causing oxidative stress.
Astringent: Mullein tea has astringent properties, which can help tighten pores and reduce oil production, making it less likely for acne-causing bacteria to thrive.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Verbascum thapsus, also known as mullein, teas helps with acne because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The tea contains saponins and flavonoids that combat acne-causing bacteria, reducing inflammation and preventing future breakouts. Additionally, the tea's antioxidant properties help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and promoting a more even skin tone. By incorporating mullein tea into your skincare routine, you may experience a reduction in acne severity and improved overall skin health. Its natural properties make it an attractive alternative to harsh acne treatments.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: Iridoid glycosides have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and inflammation associated with acne.
Saponins: Saponins have been shown to possess antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help combat the bacteria that cause acne.

Phenolic acids (rosmarinic acid): Phenolic acids, particularly rosmarinic acid, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to acne.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used for their anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce acne inflammation.
Flowers: They contain antiseptic and antibacterial properties that help combat acne-causing bacteria.
Buds: They are used for their astringent properties that help reduce acne and prevent further breakouts.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh verbascum thapsus flowers in the early morning to preserve their potency and aroma.
Dry the verbascum thapsus flowers in a warm and dry place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Chop 2-3 teaspoons of dried verbascum thapsus flowers into small pieces to increase surface area for infusion.
Steep 1 teaspoon of chopped verbascum thapsus flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the verbascum thapsus tea and discard the solids to enjoy the herbal tea as a calming beverage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Devil's claw teas helps with acne because it contains harpagoside, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. This property can help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne lesions, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing the skin. Additionally, devil's claw tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help combat free radicals that can cause oxidative stress, a known contributor to acne development. By drinking devil's claw tea regularly, individuals may be able to reduce their acne symptoms and achieve clearer, healthier-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Devil's claw contains anti-inflammatory compounds like harpagosides, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne.
Antibacterial properties: The plant's extracts have been shown to exhibit antibacterial activity against certain types of bacteria that can cause acne, such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes).

Antioxidant properties: Devil's claw tea is rich in antioxidants, which help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the appearance of fine lines, wrinkles, and other signs of skin aging.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with acne.
Rhizomes: Rhizomes are rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria, a common cause of acne.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried harpagophytum procumbens root powder into a clean tea infuser or strainer.
Boil fresh water in a teapot and pour it over the herb, allowing it to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large cup, discarding any solids remaining behind.
Add one tablespoon of raw honey to the tea and stir until dissolved, sweetening the brew to taste.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking, sipping slowly throughout the day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild potato teas helps with acne because of its unique combination of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and antimicrobial properties. The tea's active ingredients work together to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that can cause breakouts. Additionally, the antioxidants help to neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and lead to acne. By drinking wild potato tea regularly, individuals can promote healthy skin and reduce the appearance of acne.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Wild potato tea's antibacterial properties help combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a type of bacteria that can cause acne by reducing inflammation and preventing bacterial overgrowth.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in wild potato tea, such as flavonoids and saponins, help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, making it an effective natural remedy for calming the skin.

Antioxidant properties: Wild potato tea's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to the formation of acne lesions, promoting healthier and clearer skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in starch and fiber, the roots help to absorb excess oil and reduce inflammation in the skin, making it an effective ingredient in teas for acne treatment.
Rhyzomes: With its anti-inflammatory properties, rhyzomes can help soothe redness and irritation caused by acne, promoting a clearer complexion.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried taraxacum kok saghyz leaves for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Combine the measured leaves and water in a teapot or infuser, letting it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids.
Add one tablespoon of honey to the tea, stirring until dissolved, and let it cool to room temperature.
Store the tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days or drink immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Canada thistle teas helps with acne because its anti-inflammatory properties can reduce redness and swelling associated with breakouts. The tea's antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, also help to combat free radicals that can contribute to skin damage and acne. Additionally, the tea may help to regulate hormonal imbalances, which are often linked to acne-prone skin. By promoting overall skin health and reducing inflammation, Canada thistle teas may provide a natural solution for managing acne and achieving clearer, healthier-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Saponins: Saponins have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce acne-causing bacteria on the skin and alleviate redness and swelling associated with breakouts.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids are antioxidants that can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to acne development.
Taraxasterol: Taraxasterol has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help reduce the severity of acne by inhibiting the growth of bacteria that cause breakouts.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Canada thistle contain compounds that have been shown to reduce inflammation and combat bacteria, making them effective in treating acne-causing infections.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes (underground stems) of the plant contain anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help soothe and heal acne-prone skin.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried cirsium arvense flowers and 4 cups of boiling water.
Steep the dried flowers in hot water for 10-15 minutes, allowing them to infuse their medicinal properties.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large pitcher, discarding solids.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature and store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.
Pour 1-2 cups of cooled tea into a glass filled with ice and enjoy as a refreshing drink two times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright teas helps with acne because it contains antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and antibacterial properties that work synergistically to soothe and calm the skin. The tea's ability to reduce inflammation can help to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, while its antibacterial properties target and eliminate P. acnes bacteria that cause breakouts. Additionally, eyebright's antiseptic properties help to prevent further infection and promote healthy healing of acne lesions, leading to clearer, more balanced skin.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Eyebright tea contains antibacterial compounds that help combat P. acnes bacteria, which can contribute to acne formation and inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Eyebright's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with acne, promoting a faster healing process and reducing the risk of post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation (PIH).

Antioxidant properties: Eyebright tea's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to acne development, while also protecting the skin from environmental stressors.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the eyebright plant are used to make medicinal teas for acne due to their high concentration of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: Eyebright buds are rich in glycosides and phenolic compounds that help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial overgrowth, making them a popular choice for acne treatments.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried euphrasia officinalis leaves or 2 teaspoons of fresh leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Pour the measured euphrasia officinalis leaves into a large teapot or heat-resistant cup.
Bring filtered or spring water to a boil and then pour it over the euphrasia officinalis leaves, allowing them to steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean teapot or mug, discarding the solids.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature before applying it topically as a toner or compress, using a cotton ball or gauze pad.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain teas helps with acne because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which work together to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried plantago major leaves in quantities sufficient for tea preparation.
Rinse the harvested plantago major leaves thoroughly with filtered water to remove any impurities.
Combine one teaspoon of dried plantago major leaves or two teaspoons of fresh leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, allowing the herbal properties to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea solution through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve to remove any remaining plant material and enjoy as a calming beverage.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of fresh gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems or dried herb equivalent.
Boil 4 cups of filtered water in a pot and then remove from heat source immediately.
Add the harvested gaultheria procumbens herb to the boiling water, allowing it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding solids completely.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature before refrigerating it and consuming within 24 hours.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh bupleurum falcatum roots and clean them thoroughly with cold water.
Dry the cleaned roots in a warm oven at 150°f for 24 hours or air dry.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried bupleurum falcatum roots per cup of boiling water and steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea leaves and discard the solids before serving and store leftover tea in an airtight container.
Drink 2-3 cups of the prepared tea daily to help alleviate acne symptoms within a week.

remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried symphytum officinale leaves into a heat-resistant cup.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to acne that are treated with herbal teas are eczema, rosacea, and dermatitis. Herbal teas such as chamomile, calendula, and peppermint have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe itchy skin and reduce inflammation. Green tea has antioxidant properties that can help prevent damage caused by free radicals, while burdock root tea is believed to purify the blood and unclog pores, reducing symptoms of acne-like conditions.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for acne are a natural and effective way to treat this common skin condition. They work by reducing inflammation, fighting bacteria, and soothing the skin, ultimately preventing breakouts and promoting healthy skin. Some of the best herbal teas for acne include Silybum marianum, Calendula officinalis, Aloe barbadensis, Aloe vera, Melaleuca alternifolia, Echinacea purpurea, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Cymbopogon citratus, Lavandula angustifolia, and Camellia sinensis. If left untreated, acne can lead to low self-esteem, anxiety, and even depression, making everyday activities like socializing and working challenging.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with acne is a blend of chamomile, calendula, and green tea. Chamomile soothes and calms the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation. Calendula's antibacterial properties combat acne-causing bacteria, while green tea's antioxidants help to reduce inflammation and promote skin clarity. By drinking a combination of these teas, you can help to balance your skin's natural pH and reduce the appearance of acne, leading to a clearer and more radiant complexion. This natural approach can also reduce the need for harsh skincare products.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Evidence-based complementary and alternative medicine: eCAM, the use of herbal medicine as a complementary and alternative treatment for acne vulgaris is a promising strategy. This study included 34 clinical trials involving 1753 participants, which showed that herbal medicine significantly reduces inflammatory and noninflammatory acne lesions and has a relevant effect on acne severity, with some medicinal plants revealing equal or higher efficacy to standard treatments. Overall, the results of this systematic review provide evidence that herbal teas, which are often made from these medicinal plants, can be a useful addition to conventional treatments for mild to moderate acne vulgaris.
